reviews.review_msg: Scott is one of the best profs I have had at Beedie. He was so caring and would really provide opportunities for students to excel. The videos he records must have taken him so much time and he had such a professional set up for online classes I really respected him. He had a kid during the term and was still able to be accessible. Highly recommend

I took both BUS 254 and 251 with Scott and he is by far the best prof at SFU, I don't necessarily have an interest in accounting but watching his pre-recorded lectures are informative and enjoyable. Exams are a time crunch however they are fair I understand all accounting exams are like that regardless of the prof. Very caring and accessible.

I never write reviews but Raymond is so unimaginably inadequate, you must be warned before taking any class with him. he takes 254, an otherwise easy class, distorts it and makes everything seem more complicated than it is. The book gives clearer methods, yet he will profusely argue his incorrect answers despite the answer key proving him wrong.

Raymond seems very knowledgeable and passionate about accounting, however he is not a great teacher.  It is very difficult to stay focused during lectures and his explanations in tutorial are lengthy and often very confusing.

This prof is so baffling. I have never met such an inadequate teacher in my life. He will go out of his way to disagree with the answer key in the textbook. There was one question where the entire class argued with him because he disagreed with the right answer. His exams have nothing to do with his homework. He contradicts himself a lot.
Professor Zhang is very caring towards his students, very funny but if I'm being honest I learned all course material by myself. His tests were very fair though so I would recommend him!

Ray is a genuinely nice prof and he is willing to listen to feedback. He is also a lenient marker. However, he can be very disorganized and doesn't explain concepts clearly. The midterm and final were extremely fair and there are a lot of assignments, but they ultimately help you improve your understanding.

The course content isnt very hard and hes very good at giving similar types of questions on practice exams as they would be on the tests. teaching isnt anything special its pretty average but he genuinely cares for his students and tried his best to make this a good experience for his students.
Lectures were enjoyable. Midterm was reasonable
Absolute gem. Funny and better than other dry accounting profs

This is a really sarcastic prof, who uses quite a bit of dry humour. I don't think he teaches anything special, but he makes the lectures quite enjoyable. lmao, he even uses the basic ass slides from wiley plus, or his ubc slides when he taught managerial accounting at ubc. Simply put, he makes learning the material enjoyable in class.

Safia explained concepts very well and included in-class participation to keep the class engaged during lecture. Her exams are very fair and only tested the main concepts taught in the course. The homework she assigns is useful to help understand concepts taught in lecture, and serves as great practice when preparing for exams.

Safia is really nice and gives super fair exams; nothing that you didn't learn. Her voice is very calming and she tries to engage the class with participation questions that aren't for marks so you can practice. I found the course itself to be very difficult and will require lots of practice beyond homework to do well.
Safia is very fair in her exams and grading. She does not expect you to memorize everything, just key concepts. She gives great practice questions that prepare you very well for quizzes and exams.
Safia is great professor. It was very nice to have someone with real life experience teach accounting. I don't plan on being an accountant but Safia made the course material interesting and fun!

Great prof with actual experience in the field. Does a great job of explaining concepts with real life examples that he has been through. Notes are simple and to the point. Tests are very fair and simple if you do the textbook assignments. Grade is based on tutorial, midterm, and final which makes tutorial very important, I learned the hard way.
Awesome prof! Really experienced so he's great at explaining and giving examples. Focus in class and then read through textbook! Test heavy so don't cram, you need to study well in advance.

One of the easiest courses to follow as there it only lectured which follow the book step by step, and tutorials on pre-done exercises - amen! However, it is a quantitative course and you need to get your head around it. Marco is great; clear, precise, motivating, and humble. You won't regret the course even if you aren't a numerical person.
Marco is a really good prof. His exams are fair but since it is a quantitative course doing the homework questions for tutorials and understanding the concepts is fundamental.
Great Prof!
Awful experience all round.  Very difficult to understand. Apparently she's done at SFU and moving on to another school! A blessing for sure!!
Do not take this course with Tanya Tang. You will regret it.  If you like equations then I guess she is for you, if you are into theory, take it with Kamlani.  Period.

Very hard to understand at first, but once your able to translate what she is saying, its not so bad.  Reads straight from slides, but still have to go to class for pop quizzes. Overall, it's a challenging course, doesn't matter who the prof is.  Tanya, although difficult to understand, is very nice and does try her best to help students learn.

She is a terrible professor. Has no clue how to teach and makes no attempt to better herself. Her sample midterms are not remotely close to the exams. It is purely a self taught class, because she relies on the manuf. powerpoint throughout the class, and has nothing to add!
I thought she was good. I love Lyryx! Totally helped me. This class just seemed to make sense!

most horrible teacher ever, someone who cant speak english should not be teaching at a university where we speak english.  you need a translator to understand what she says.  she struggles over words and its like she doesnt understand her powerpoints. some pointers: when she says "causes" its really cost.and we use costs a lot

Wow she is the worst teacher on earth. As mentioned before she cannot speaky english. Wow and another thing she is freaking annoying. I hate when she tries to pronounce words such as labour. She practically reads off the slides and expects us to do well. I think she is a bit crazy too. She is unreasonable and is sad to look at and sad to listentoo
He is a great prof! Sometimes he can go off topic but otherwise really good as he is approachable and willing to answer questions.

When I took BUS254 in summer 08 it was Mary's first time teaching. For a first timer, I thought she was pretty good. Midterm was fairly easy--the average was 75%. The final however was more on the challenging side.

Mary is an awesome professor. She made the course easy to understand. Her midterms and the final are fair. Make sure you do the tutorial questions and understand them! I'd definately take another class with her!

Excellent professor.  Her notes are very useful and clear. She communicates her materials very fluently.   However, her exams are hard and time consuming, especially the final. Overall, she is a great accounting professor. I would rate her 4.5/5.

Her lectures are very clear and straightforward. The lecture notes are useful and clear in every sense and somehow u dun even needa read the textbook if u pay attention in class. The midterm is easy but the final is a bit challenging. But overall as an accounting prof, she explains everything clearly n makes everything easy and simply. Recommended!

Was very rude and inefficient during the midterm. Assigns useless homework. Exams were nothing like the homework or tutorial problems. Tutorials were rushed and not helpful. Often left lectures feeling confused. Learned more with 2 hours of YouTube videos than I did a whole semester of lectures. No support/guide for exams.

Course was horribly mismanaged by this professor. His lectures were fine and he explained topics relatively clearly but antisocial TAs had no idea what they were doing during tutorials. They would give 90 minutes worth of questions to do in 30 minutes. Prof took midterm wayy too seriously, spent exam time preparing room and gave no extra time.
Terrible teaching style. Goes through lectures quickly with no explanation. No practice sheet given out to prepare for midterm. No help what so ever.

Professor gives little to no explanation on course topics and expects students to understand lectures while zooming through nearly 100 lecture slides per class. Midterm/Final was nothing like the homework he assigns through WILEY, and provides no means of study material for exams or any feedback on assignments done through homework or tutorial.

This guy makes SFU look bad. He has such a huge ego and the fact that he is allowed to have responsibility over student's grades should be criminal given how little he cares for them, and once again weakens the schools reputation.

Worst prof I have ever met. He does not seem to care about his students, even if they reached out to him. He does not seem to want his students to succeed. You're better off taking a course with another prof.
WHAT A WASTE OF TIME!! PLEASE DON'T TAKE IT FROM HIM
Don't take his class!!! He is so bad! I don't understand how he is still allowed to teach!

AVOID!!! The worst prof I've ever had. At first, he seems like a nice person but that's not how he actually is. He thinks that everyone is cheating because they did better on the final exam than the midterm. It's my first time posting a review here because everyone should know how bad he is. Taking BUS 254 with him was the biggest mistake I made.

He is USELESS!! AVOID THIS PROF AT ALL COSTS. Looking for a professor that drastically lowers your GPA? Micheal is your man :) I would rather take all the courses at SFU simultaneously than taking any course with this professor individually. NOTE: If you read any positive reviews about him don't believe it. He would have written it himself.
Horrible. Don't take. Save yourself.

Worst prof I have ever had at SFU, I have never felt so disrespected. Homework was absurdly difficult at times and he is difficult to talk to. Exams killed me he does not post his slides. Avoid at all costs!!!
Seems like a nice guy at first but really does not care about his students at all. He makes his own rules that may not be even apart of university policy.

Very unorganized with everything. Lecture slides posted on Canvas don't match the ones used in class and he refused to post them. Talks fast and lectures are hard to follow. For exams I just studied the textbook and redo the hw questions.
shouldnt have got hired
lectures and tuturials were uncoordinated
Midterm and final was unfair. Way too much homework

This was the worst prof I have ever had. Unclear in lectures, horrible at teaching, gave an absolutely absurd amount of homework that was worth a lot. Ive been at sfu for 3 years, this is the first prof Ive ever rated because it just had to be known how unbelievably bad he was. I tried to rate him a 0/5 but couldnt.
literally his lectures and his homework qn/ tutorials are completely off...

Michael's class had such a large disconnect between lecture material, homework questions, and tutorial work that it was almost impossible to grasp key concepts. What makes matters worse is that the in-class slides and the tutorial work could not be viewed after said class/tutorials and so if your note-taking isn't up to par, you're done for.

Online homework was pretty frustrating - if you got one part wrong you could lose 1/3 of the points. Not sure how people did well on hw. Lectures were decent, short and as interesting as accounting can be. Tutorials were useful and well organized. Only one hard question on the midterm the rests of the tests weren't too bad. Overall a decent class.
Very enthusiastic, makes the class fun. Passionate about the subject and very knowledgeable.
dry sense of humour, refuses to provide answers for textbook questions, blunt and rude. Probably one of the most difficult classes I've taken.

Lectures were useless, basically just read off the slides the whole time, which came straight out of the textbook. He did go through some practice problems as examples but he goes so fast there's no way to know what's going on. Beware the midterm.

HARDEST PROF AT SFU!! hands down, 3rd year student who kept delaying 254 cuz this guy was always teaching it... Youre gonna fail the midterm.. Final is more fair but extremely tough. Take 254 with a better prof in summer. Don't take this advice and youll get a C...

Michael is an all around really great person, he has a quirky sense of humour and is there for students when needed. However,  to do well in this course you really need to put the time in, homework is difficult and seeing the T.A's to understand it is essential. Midterm and final were relatively tough so do all of the homework and lecture Q's.

His exams are tough but if you practice and understand the concepts you will do fine.  Attendance to tutorial is mandatory and you are required to do a presentation on homework assignment.  He is very friendly and helpful in his office hours and it is clear he wants to help you succeed.  If you are confused on anything go to his office hours.

Meh. Not that great, not the worst though. His lecture slides could be a lot better, but at least he gets all the concepts across decently enough. Make sure you do all the homework assignments, or else you'll definitely fall behind.
Prof is terrible, final exam doesn't explain material well at all. His powerpoint notes are terrible. Avoid Him!
Easily my favorite Beedie prof. He's direct, clear and super funny! Definitely takes the dryness out of accounting

I absolutely love this guy!! His blunt sense of humour is fantastic and it keeps the class interesting. I had a low interest on accounting and to be honest was a little nervous at the beginning of the semester. The txtbook he wrote is really useful in understnding the topics. As long as you keep up with readings and doing the questions you'll be ok

This prof is not helpful. His lectures are not very organized and he doesnt explain the material very well. He goes through some problems in class but does not really explain it.. He just does it on the overhead. I would avoid his lectures if possible
Great Prof. Exams are much easier than the weekly questions, so if you keep up with those you'll be fine. Interesting guy too, he knows his stuff
I don't do RMP comments, but his marking style was so bad... that i had to do it...
Really nice prof! Midterms and finals are not  that hard as his book's excercises. His joke about ROI is funny! Love him!
He has a really nice personality, and the book is pretty straight forward. His exams don't look like any of the practice questions, but they still made sense.

ok so on the final and MT he decides to throw in q's we've nvr seen in class or assgmts, totally unfair!! he was apparently writing a book so the lectures in class were not the same as what he posted online due to copyright issues, gimme a break. AVOID HIM AT ALL COST!!!

Great guy, quite funny, which keeps lectures interesting. Clear explanations, just like the textbook he wrote. Like all other business profs, he shows u clearly the percentage of the class that gets a certain grade, don't know how weird that can be.

He has a good sense of humor, which sometimes keeps me awake. Yes, accounting is so boring. It isn't hard, as long as you read textbook, you'll survive. His marking policy is very strange. I should have a A+ though.
boring lecturer, came off as a little arrogant, went through concepts too quickly, didn't learn anything from him - had to learn it all from textbook myself :(

ridiculous. 60 something ppl got Cs. that's right, not C+. only a few ppl got b- and a- or b+. weirdest curve EVER. i got 4% above average as my final grade and i still got a C. how the hell does that work?!

Lectures aren't mandatory but they help to understand the material. Lots of work! There are weekly assignments for tutorial as well as online assignments that can take a long time. Midterm was brutal! But for some reason the final exam was MUCH easier. The curve is a little ridiculous, it gave way too many students Bs and not enough B+ or A-
in an accounting class over 180 students, he only gave less than 4 students A+'s after the class is curved. That's too mean!

Used his own PPT notes from a book he penned that will be published in Fall. Since they were "copyrighted by the publisher," he could not send them to us. This was particularly unhelpful. Not a hard course really, but lecture style made topics dry and boring. Attendance not really mandatory, just read the book. Mcdonald did a far better job in 251!

This class is HARD and this guy makes it even harder for some students. His midterm was a KILLER. like omg, the average was 52%, but his final was easy. overall, he is good at explaining concepts but  he is just wayyy too hard. he doesnt even have lecture slides for you because he is making his own book or something. well, BS, thats complete garbag

Worst Professor I have ever had in three years.  No notes, not helpful and is a complete jerk when you ask him for help. The average for midterm was 52%.  Makes you write in pen for accounting!.  Honestly the worst professor I have ever had

Friendly enough but the amount of content covered is too much and the amount of work weekly is way too much. No Powerpoint posted because he's writing a book but the ones he uses are useless. He flips between them so fast, you can't follow anyways.

Worst Accounting Professor and maybe professor Ive ever had! Tests were unfair ( tested us and material not lectured upon or in text) NO NOTES because he is making his own book therefore can't print out his notes or use the text and goes through slides to fast to let you copy anything down.  AVOID AT ALL COSTS GPA KILLER and HORRIBLE PROFESSOR
Good Prof. Taught well and was very thorough. Ensure you study hard though, becasuse it's not an easy course.
The final was tricky.  The powerpoint notes are the official ones from the textbook, but would wish they were shorter.
This prof is a moron. Yes his lectures may be interesting but it doesn't do crap on the exams. The course is interesting but the prof made the course crap. I think this prof should be fired.

I think he got mad because people left class before it ends. The reason? cuz he was doing some stupid demonstration problems after the lecture. There is no break in his lecture, and if you go to see him personally and asks some questions, he would repeat things in your text. It was not a bad course but he made it bad. Final was crazy brutal.

Another example of a nice guy who can't teach worth crap. After the midterm, for some reason, he wouldn't let TAs answer questions on WebCt, his lectures are repeating poewrpoint slides, and he spammed the hell out of my inbox. Don't evenstudy for the final, you'll fail anyway, it's based on time management, not knowledge.

haha notice how the ratings changed from 3 and 4s to all 1s after this final..haha hilarious. i dont know if he's doing it out of revenge or was his exams always this hard. he was like the nicest guy ever but the more i get to know him the more of a d*** he becomes. i guess after we filled out the evaluations he turned the tables on us.pretty slick

He may sound clear during lectures, and make everything seem so easy, until the final exam comes. The final exam was the worst, hardest, trickest, and longest final exam ever, after my three years at SFU.

He disliked teaching this course and it showed after the midterm.  It was almost like he deter people from accounting.  Definately take this course with another prof, even though apparently he's never teaching a 200 level course again.
He is very experienced and it shows when he gives examples in lectures. He makes lectures interesting ... and his exams are fair.
Great Prof, great learning, study hard!
great prof, i enjoyed being in his class. really knows the subject and enjoys teaching it. always available for extra help! exams are a little hard but fair
class was so boring, and his powerpoint notes are not much help either. Everyone does well so it all depends on final, which by the way was brutal!

VERY boring class, though helpful--lots of interaction on WebCT. Midterm was easy, final was harder. He should release distributions--nothing worse than not knowing where u are in a curved course when u thought the exams were super easy. Seriously if you can do arithmetic, u will pwn this boring course. And ignore his nonsense about the appendices

He is a good individual(helpful) If you're interested in accounting/business, youll find course useful and easy but for others it will be very confusing. ppl tend to do well in this class (summer semstr average was in 70-80s!) i am glad it is over even though he is a nice guy
His teaches very clearly, but the problem is everyone is good at this course.  Therefore, it is still hard to get high marks.
He is nice and has interesting view. He is a good prof.

He breezes through the Powerpoints so you can't take notes, and he won't post them neither.  There are old pwrpnts but they're a bit different than the one's he's using.  Examples he goes through in class really easy, Exam questions really difficult..
Great prof and great class!
His teaching ability is a kind of weak because he just read what it's on the lecture notes. But his exams are not bad, quite straight foward. Hope he can be friendly in next semester.
Cool accent, but that's the only thing good about this guy. I really hated the course and breathed a huge sigh of relief when it was over. HARD exams!
Funny, nice guy, but not that great of the teacher.  Cool accent.
terrible communicator
One of the worst profs ever!  He regurgitates what the textbook said and he is not helpful at all.  Avoid him if you can!
His lecture is fun and easy to understand but the content itself is very hard.  You need to do all the questions in the exercise book if you want a good mark.
No Comments
This prof doesn't answer any question, and doesn't allow TA to answer hw questions too.
No Comments

EASY class. Exams were ridiculously easy. If you understood the material and homework questions, u would have no problem on exams. Lectures were pointless, just look at the textbook examples before attempting weekly homework and ur set.

Bob is a nice guy so it makes it hard to rag on him. But he can not teach. It is nearly impossible to pay attention & you will take nothing away from lecture. But he wants the class to do well and gives the easiest exams you'll ever write. Averages were 75%. I learned everything the night before the exams by doing his practice qsts and got an A+.
Nice prof, knows the material very well but lectures can be a bit dry. Exams are not difficult, just make sure you read the textbook and do the practice questions.

Midterm and final written was fair b/c he lets you know what's on it. MC is tricky, calculation MC was straightforward. Can't teach, but such a great guy! Lectures and text don't help. Chapter questions much more difficult than on exams. Take it with him to get a good grade, but don't expect to learn much. Gestures and speech...absolutely hilarious

Decent prof. Not super engaging, but what do you expect when accounting is the subject? Really nice guy, very laid back and doesn't want you to feel any stress from his class. Multiple choice on midterm + final were both challenging, with the calculation M/C and written parts being pretty straightforward.

The lectures were very dry and hard to follow. Anderson does not do a good job of communicating in a way that keeps the students interested. He tends to be quite repetitive and mumbles a lot of jibberish. One of the worst profs i've ever had. Not recommended.

Really good prof, no surprises on his exam and tell you before hand exactly what is going to be on the exams. Just read ahead and do tutorial questions and sample exam and you will be fine. Very understanding as well. Course load is not heavy and is not difficult nor easy its average.

He's one of the nicest, most approachable, and kind profs I've had. He speeds through lectures but he will go to great lengths to make sure his students do well. He will always be available for extra time and will sit through any amount of problems to help you. His exams are tricky, but they're fair. HIGHLY recommended, amazing guy!

Richard is a nice guy and a decent teacher. His exams are much harder than the practise material given due to numerous trick questions, I did decent in the course but felt ill prepared for all exams even tho I studied all the practise questions he provides.

I had 254, he's a great guy and is straight up that this course and formulas is annoying but not impossible.I will say his sample practice for midterms and exams are not similar on what to expect. Exams were really hard, because of his trick calculation questions which took up a lot of time. I would practice questions on textbook. Overall good guy!
The midterm and final are definitely harder than the practice questions, tutorials, and slides. But he's a great guy. I really like him. I used to hate accounting, but he inspires me a lot.
Engaging lectures and easy to understand/ approach prof. Finally passed 254 on this 3rd attempt
Richard Wong is impressive! His teaching method is the best that I've experienced so far. Make sure you do enough practice, then an A is easy to achieve.

Richard is fun, passionate, and clearly loves teaching. That said, his exams are brutal. The midterms and finals feel harder than many STEM exams I've taken. He writes original questions, so tutorial and extra practice problems do not reflect the test well. Be prepared for a very HARD time.

Took 254 with Richard this semester after reading his great reviews; don't listen to them. The midterm exam was much different from the in class pratice and tutorial work. The final was brutal; I had to remember 30 formulas off the top of my head. On top of that, it was 25 pages long, and all the MC were calculations. Be ready to study extra hard!
Good good good! Amzing Prof ever met
Took 251 with Richard, he is the best Prof I had at SFU Beedie! So excited to choose his 254 for next term.
Steve is one of the better profs that teaches BUS 254. His lectures will be boring at times but he is somewhat funny as well. The exams are tiring but are not too difficult. Overall, not a bad prof.
Steve is ok but his lecture was unorganized. Hard marking on the exam.. Would not take again with him

used to love accounting but totally hate it now.worst & most boring prof ever.Not on campus a week before the finals.Don't go to lectures as they are useless.His powerpoint slides,which he only reads are taken from the CD at the back of the book.No point in going to lectures,just read the textbook.

Lectures frequently ended early, midterm average exactly 50% before scaling, said "thats not my fault", gave hints to what would be on exams, but was much more difficult than expected. Not helpful during office hours, syllabus wording required multiple instances of clarification, reads lecture slides for the first time. Avoid her like the plague.

Before taking 251 for A with Richard. That man was the best prof I met at SFU Beedie. Never thought this easier course could let her teach like that. I have seen the GOAT will start to teach 254 from next term, ready to retake and learn this course again.

Told us in lecture what would be on the final exam, then put the complete opposite it. Questions on the final were nothing like the homework. The average for the class was practically a failing mark. If you want to tank your gpa, I'd recommend taking this class with Susan.

Reasons to not take this class: 

- For our 10:30am lectures her office hours were 9:30am-10:30am however I would see her leave her car in the parking lot and walk straight to lecture

- During lectures Susan would “see this slide for the first time” meaning she wouldn't even know what she's teaching 

Catastrophic Prof
She is sucks at teaching.
DON'T TAKE ANY CLASSES WITH HER!!!!
If you failed, she will laugh and proud. If you passed, she will give as low as you can on your final letter grades.
If you want to fail, welcome to choose her course!
Her teaching skills are completely inadequate. There was no interaction at all with the students. If you happened to take her course, you can only hope for the pass.

We would ask our TA questions about the class and they would respond with I don't know because Susan doesn't tell me anything. Her final exam was genuinely so different from the homework, I saw questions that I had never seen before in the HW. Midterm class average was a 50%.
this professor put me back on antidepressants ONG DONT TAKE HER
she gets off on seeing students fail
if you know mean girls movie, lets just say add Susan to the cover of your burn book
if you're not religious pick a religion and pray
I love him so much, hes my fav! I dont usually give a rating or comment, just read and leave. But Weiming Liu is the best out of the best! He makes SFU and Accounting great again! Thanks

Weiming made BUS 254 an approachable course. He explained concepts clearly and welcomed any questions we had. Attendance is necessary because of the marked in-class group quizzes but the question pools for them are in the syllabus. His exams were super fair and reflected the homework and tutorial problems. He also allowed formula sheets on exams.
It is not an easy course, to be honest; he is a nice instructor and always willing to help his students. But the exams are pretty easy and straightforward, so choose him confidently!
His class is so disorganized and never know how Beedie could have such instructor like him, better to take with other profs
Very good professor, passionate about the topics, midterm was fairly straightforward, but final was extremely lengthy and required a broad understanding of topics, make sure to study hard!

before this i absolutely hated accounting, but dr.tan actually made me like it. she clearly explains concepts. even though she's old school, her mandatory weekly homework and lecture notes are really helpful! make sure you participate in tutorials. do all the hw questions to prep for the exam. tip: go to TA office hours!! Gui was the best TA

Dr Tan is an excellent professor. Both exams are similar the homework and practice questions, so as long as you consistently practice them, you should perform well.

Don't be scared to go to office hours, she wants to help you succeed. 


Tips: 
- Participate in the lectures and tutorials
- Spam practice the homework questions before the exams

Exams are very tough, but so is accounting. The exams were a fair reflection of the course content. If you know the material, youll do well relative to the curve (Class average in my section was about 65% for the whole course). Dr Tan is awesome, clear explanations, and assigns ample practice questions. If you work hard, youll do well.

Dr Tan is a very straight forward prof. If you go to lectures and do all the homework/additional practices question you are set up for success in her exams. Her final was quite long and weighted heavily, but was doable if you thoroughly practice how she tells you to. She does a good job of simplifying textbook content, so lectures are all you need.

Dr. Tan genuinely cares about this class. The exam questions were exactly as the quizzes/homework so if you lock into those you will be just fine. Even though the final is 50%, tutorial quizzes/homework add up big time to around 20% of your grade. If you get Gui as your TA he is awesome and was probably the main reason I did good on the exams.

MT and Final were both like the practice Qs she assigns. if you can do ALL the questions she gives youll be fine. Beware, her exams are very long and difficult, you have to know the material to finish the exam on time. HW check and quiz in tut (30% of grade), 20%MT and 50% Final. Expect a curve boost, av was about 60% on the MT and 55% on final.

Hwee Tan was not a good professor by any means. She tries too hard to "Simplify" concepts but realistically all it does is either confuse you more or make you underestimate the actual material. Do the homework and additional practices for Midterm and Final and you will do fine. But don't expect much from her lectures waste of time to be honest.

Dr Tan is an amazing prof and getting a good grade is achievable without going to lectures or reading the textbook. Just make sure to show up to tutorial and do the homework. Anyone saying that the final is nothing like we've seen before just got IQ pilled. The final is exactly like the homework, and there is nothing different.

Class wasn't too hard. She's a kind and good prof. I didn't show up to lectures, but the tutorial is mandatory. She only asks questions about things you should know. You won't do badly in the class if you practice the questions, and just IQ pill the tests. Final is long, but you should be able to finish if you actually know the stuff.

Anyone saying the final was nothing like we've seen before is coping. I didn't go to many lectures, just studied them at home and did all the homework questions (truly all you need), and IQ diffed everyone else at the final exam ig.

hw check every week with a group quiz in tutorials. the mt was only 2hrs and had similar questions like the hw and group quiz, but the final was 3 hrs. looked nothing like anything we've seen before. all exams were very tight on time and everyone stayed to the last minute. had to sacrifice some mcq to get through all the written questions.

I had a 96% coming out of the midterm so may be biased, but Tan is a sweet lady. Lectures not mandatory but slides & accounting itself are hard to follow from home. Tuts mandatory. Midterm was pre much exactly like the hw, final was not. Myself and 90% of the class did not finish the final in time. If you have to take Tan, I wouldnt stress it.

Hwee is a kind person, however not a good professor. Her teaching style is very old school and her slides are difficult to comprehend. The final exam worth 50% was extremely difficult, and no one was done on time.

The final was worth 50 percent and was one of the most brutal ones I have written. The questions that are available for tutorial and lectures are relaxed and easily understandable but the final was hella gruesome, it was 3 hours long and yet felt like there wasn't enough time to finish.

Midterms & exams are WAY harder than what she makes you think (and her hints are low-key bad and don't help with studying at all). Not enough time with midterms and exams... everyone in the exam was there until the VERY END. Make sure you have a good group for the quizzes; otherwise, you will be below the average in the course.

EVERYTHING IS ON PAPER. MIDTERMS AND EXAMS ARE SUPER HARD AND NOTHING LIKE HER PRACTICE QUESTIONS. GROUP QUIZZES SOLELY DEPEND ON IF YOU HAVE A GOOD GROUP OR NOT. DOESN'T GIVE ENOUGH TIME FOR HER MIDTERMS AND EXAMS... ENDS UP BLAMING YOU FOR BAD TIME MANAGEMENT EVEN THOUGH SHE FINISHES LECTURES LATE AND EVERYONE IN THE CLASS DOESN'T FINISH no joke

If I could tell my past self one thing about this class, it would be to try my absolute best on everything before the final. Participate in tutorials every day, do good in tutorial quizzes and most importantly, the midterm. I am saying this because of the difficulty of the final exam, which will contain questions you have not seen before.

Hwee is a good prof who genuinely cares about her students' learning. Her lectures can be boring, but after all, it is accounting. She provided more than enough resources and help to succeed in this class. Overall, she's a lovely person and wants to see you do well. Her exams were very hard, but the course is curved. Do better than most, get an A.

prof's tests are not like the practice at all, they are way too long for the time given and much more difficult. the course overall is challenging, however, the exams were made far more challenging than necessary

Dr. Tan was good at presenting the material in a easy way to understand whether or not you are going into accounting. Homework felt meaningful to do, but note that the Speedy Guide will only be as useful as you make it. Big chunk of grading is on midterm/final, and the biggest issue is answering an unnecessary large # of questions in only 2-3 hrs.

Didn't appreciate prof's grading style; requires you to do homework according to her style instead of your own learning style. weekly quizzes contain different content than HW and exams are so strictly graded that you must know the exact formats of like 8 different budgets studied in each chapter. got an A in bus251 but am not doing well with Hwee.
I'm not a ppl who really into accounting, but Tan is actually make everything easier. Although she's lecture pace is slow but she will be super patient and helpful if you ask her any questions.

She gave us chocolate on the first day of lecture, amazing first lecture! She clearly is very knowledgable and cares for all students. She is funny and uses great informative examples of real-life situations to explain concepts.
Great prof would take again

Dr Tan was a fantastic professor. She was very passionate about the subject material and really cared for her students and wanted them to do well. Dr Tan's exams were difficult, especially the final, but she provided many resources and practice questions for them. The course was not easy, but I would 100% recommend doing it with Dr Tan.

I enjoyed taking BUS254 with Dr. Tan. She made her lectures engaging and easy to follow along. She does not require the textbook, but reading it helped me. Her exam is very similar to the homework questions, so practice those. There are group quizzes and homework checks during tutorial, but these assignments are very straightforward.

I LOVED BUS254 with Dr Tan. Her lectures are extremely informative and cover all content needed for the class making it actually worth it to go to class each week. There is weekly written homework and group quizzes. Only thing I didn't like was that the final (58%) was worth WAY more than the midterm (19%).

The course is hard and there is a lot of material to learn but Dr. Tan is kind and definitely well educated on the course. The most stressful part is the final worth 58% and midterm worth 19%. Attend all tuts so you can get marks on the weekly quizzes, participation and hw. Luckily, my TA was cool (shoutout Donovan). Study hard, it's worth it.

Hwee Tan is a good professor and is passionate about this course. She doesn't grade any of the work, but I found the TA's to be tough graders. I also found it odd that my TA gave me 0/1 on some homework checks when I completed all my work for every week.

254 was a hard course, and Dr. Tan certainly tried to make it as simple as possible. I used to hate accounting back in 251 but in 254 Dr. Tan is great with explaining concepts with examples. The material itself were a lot, by attending all lectures will help significantly. Attendance to lecture is optional but tutorial attendance are worth marks.

shes honestly an average prof, she records all her lecture which is very nice. Tutorial is mandatory, weekly hw checks, notes check, and quizzes worth 20% of your grade. does not respond to any emails, pray you have a good TA who will excuse any tuts u cant make
Very passionate and good teacher. Hard course, but Tan is great

Dr. Tan needs to revise this course. There is too much weight on exams (77%) & they are extremely difficult. The tutorials have homework every week also. She also makes us complete a useless study guide for marks.
Dr. Tan was an okay professor. She cares quite a bit about her students & wants all of them to succeed, but I was not fond of her course criteria. The 58% final & quizzes every week adds up.

BUS254 is a fairly difficult course, with a lot of homework and hard tests and exams. Therefore, some people may have put lower ratings for how hard the course is. Though, Hwee Tan is a great professor, and the homework she assigns will help you prepare for exams. Schedule accordingly as this course requires a lot of work.

Dr. Tan isn't a bad professor entirely, but her course should be revised. There's a lot of homework that takes hours to complete weekly. No big deal as this can be expected. However, most of your grade (approximately 80%) is based on the midterm and final, with the final having excessive weight. These exams are way harder than homework.

This class is the most challenging business course I've taken so far, mainly due to her surprising exams. Dr. Tan did not provide enough time during both the mid-term and the final exam, and the questions were way more challenging than the practice questions that she had provided. Tutorials are mandatory as well. Thank god this class is curved..

Dr. Tan is a very rational professor. She lays out the material in a way to be studied easily, and she provides all the resources for you to understand the content. Her sense of humour helps one learn rather than impedes it. All around a solid prof.

Dr. Tan made what might seem like complicated topics alot easier with her well thought-out lecutres.Textbook is helpful before lectures. Homework is very doable, just give yourself time and get those participation marks in tutorial for easy marks. Ask questions during lecture breaks, she's very helpful. Final is tough but midterm was okay.

The final was one of the hardest exams I've ever taken. Beware of time because she likes to put lots of questions on her exams while only providing a short time frame. Exam questions were nothing like the homework.

Dr. Tan is an amazing prof who truly cares about her students and wants them all to succeed. In tutorials, there are weekly group quizzes which can be time consuming so hopefully you get a good group. The midterm and final were hard so it's important you study everything (lectures, notes, WileyPlus practice questions, quizzes, and sample exams).

Hwee Tan is such a sweet prof but she made the midterm and final difficult. Almost all the students took the full 3hrs to finish the exam and even then I couldn't answer all the questions because some were quite hard. However, because of the curve, everyone's grade will be boosted

Dr. Tan is an amazing prof!! She genuinely tries to make students' lives easier by catering to exactly what you need, even giving you points for simple participation and hw completion. Her exams are very fair and tested directly from her lectures. To do well: Do the problem sets at the end of every chapter and mark them with the super caring TAs!

Get ready for hours of writing useless annotations and a cheat sheet which you dont even use during exams. Her exams are doable, but her homework system is frustrating asf and makes you not want to study the content. Its like a math class where you have to show your work for every single little detail, its just annoying. I wish praise ma taught 254
She's nice, caring. She made Accounting so easy and understandable. Final is pretty tough I must say. As long as you put the work and take it serious then you will get the grade you deserve.

she's the queen fr. I hate accounting but she made it make some kind of sense. If you're lost on something, she gets straight to the point and caters to your problem so that YOU get it. Genuinely encouraging & kind to students and exams are fair. Her negative reviews are cause she was a lil tough when online started but rest assured she's 5/5.

Dr. Tan is a smart woman, but her lectures are insanely dry. 71% of themark was based on the midterm and final, and the rest were through participation, weekly homework, and weekly quizzes. Homeworks take time, quizzes are done in tutorials, and full participation marks "can only be given to a few people." Prepare well for exams and you'll be fine.

254 is hard, but she does her best to make it easy. i did well on the group quizzes, hw, average on the midterm, failed the final BADLY and still got a C+ even tho the final is 46%. Lectures over textbook, she'll ask about lecture examples. final is so hard, try ur best. participate in tuts to get the 4%, and hw every week. start the hw early!

I asked for a form of accommodation and Dr Tan was unwilling to accommodate. Completely ignored my vulnerable state. I felt as if she did not understand that I was performing poorly due to personal problems and not because of my understanding of course content. I had to set aside my feelings to go from a C- to B,not allowing me to cope during COVID

Dr. Tan is an absolute gem. She is very caring and explains concepts clearly. You have weekly homework assignments that are not too hard and with each question, you have to write a reflection that will boost your homework mark. Participation in the tutorial and do the homework and the class will be smooth sailing.

Dr. Tan did a pretty good job teaching the class overall. There is quite a bit of homework that is assigned each week, but it helps you understand the course material as it is somewhat tough. There are weekly group quizzes during tutorials, but they aren't too difficult. Overall, Tan did well and it seemed like she actually cared about students.

Dr. tan is such a sweet prof. She genuinely cares about her students and tries to make the material enjoyable as hard as it is for accounting. She explains the concepts in a very straightforward that makes them easy to understand. She also adapted this semester based on the feedback she got from students last semester so her exams are a lot fairer!

Dr Tan is one of the sweetest profs ive ever had. she genuinely cares about her students and wants all of them to do well. bus254 is not an easy subject to understand, but her lectures are very clear and makes you very interested in the accounting. her exams and quizzes are fair, and youre able to do well in them if you watch her lectures.

Intro accounting concepts can be difficult to explain. Relative to other profs, Dr. Tan does a good job explaining the concepts. For my class she had all of the lectures prerecorded from fall 2019. Watch those on 1.6x speed and take notes. Much better than watching in person. Also, tutorials have a lot of group work and participation.

The prof was absolutely the worst one I have had so far, the lectures were different than the hw, the hw was different than the midterm, the midterm format was different than the final. Everything was very different. Take the prof if you are up for surprises. Good luck if you take her

Dr. Tan is such a gem! She is sweet but also strict and fair! her lectures are so easy to understand and she made me excited about accounting which is not usual. She marks fairly too. The midterm and final are also not too hard or easy! overall, great experience!

At first she seemed okay but then her midterm was NO WHERE NEAR WHAT WE WERE LEARNING. They said more than half the class failed but didn't want to give us the actual percent. In her final, she made people share their room which seemed to be a breach of privacy and made people lose time writing the exam...

I have a few things to state: 1.) Do not take this Prof at any costs. 2.) Unneccasary workload put on students by Prof. 3.) Save yourself, choose a different Prof. 4.) Her exams are no where close to how she teaches or how her practice questions are.

I just came to realize how bad this prof is. Youtube videos make more sense than her. She is very confusing and ontop makes a very confusing exam. Guys stay away from this prof, you will be so stressed after doing her notes and exams because they arent correlated at all. Notes seem so simple. Exams are 100 times harder. Be warned!!

This course has to be the worst 200 level course there is. This prof over-complicates material and has totally twisted questions on the exams. Stay away from her. Chose another prof as this one will make you frustrated to the point where you want to drop out of school. Her material and lecture notes are very long and boring. Exams are very tough!!

Prof can make exams easier but instead seems like she loves to overwhelm students with information. Not enough time and too many questions. I hope she reads this and changes her way of teaching. Test me the way you have taught on your lecture notes, stop trying to trick us. I am trying to learn the material not play games with the tricks you play.

Do not take this class with her, she may seem like a sweet and caring professor but do not be fooled. She spins everything on the exams and sends you into panic mode. I should have listened to my friend, he took her last semester and felt the same. Her exams are nothing like tutorial questions. The Prof just reads off the slides, cant teach at all!

If you are reading this please avoid her at all costs! She may seem sweet but oh geez her midterm was a nightmare. Especially during covid-19 online platform. Her homework was a walk in the park compared to the midterm. The homework is fair but the midterm was HARD. I got an A in bus 251 i shall be lucky if I pass her class due to the way she is :(

Shes hardcore. The homework she gives are too easy because the midterm she gives is much much more difficult than the homework questions. For example, questions like job cost order has too many details all over the place that you have to take into account when it comes to calculation. Her homework doesnt prepare you for the exams at all.

Dr Tan is extremely knowledgeable and knows what she's talking about. (PhD in accounting) if you ever are unsure about how to do homework, she is very helpful in office hours and you can easily ask questions in tutorials, TAs were good too. The final was very difficult and 99% of the class took the entire 3 hours, so the curve is probably low.
very hard final and not enough time

Midterm was easy. Just do tutorial questions and youll be fine. The final exam is where things went downhill. She made a super hard exam, and 3 hours was not enough to do the whole thing. Expected it to be same level of difficulty as the midterm, but the final was not like the work done in tutorials.
real hard exam

Dr. Tan is good prof. who wants her students to excel and she tries to give every resources to practice outside class including her class recording. To do well it's not necessary to attend the lecture but take advantage of the class recording and DO THE WEEKLY HOMEWORK. Exams are fairly challenging but doable.

Dr. Tan has an innate ability to communicate ideas to her students. Her lecture slides are very clear, informative and visual which are suitable for all learners. She is very approachable for help as well. Highly recommend!

Dr. Tan is a all around great lecturer who shows her interest in accounting, makes concepts very clear, organizes the material well, and prepares lectures content well. Her recordings of the lectures make it easy to go back and review any unclear concepts, but I would recommend going to lectures, as she encourages questions to ensure understanding.

Tan show her passion for teaching accounting during the lecture. and she really care about her students, she provide online recording. The difficulty of the test is fair. Try all questions after each chapter, you should be fine.
Dr. Tan is a very nice lecturer, and provides online recordings for her classes. Midterm and final was pretty hard, but if you study hard an A is achievable.

Professor Tan explains concepts well and goes more in depth in her slides. I don't find it a struggle listening to her. I think she is clear and helpful. She's really helpful on the emails. An approachable lady as well. She cares for her students and willingly answers any questions. Just study the slides, do the weekly homework, read textbook.

Tan is a good prof if you're an accounting major. If not, her class is overly complicated and requires a ton of practice and intuition. Reading lecture slides and knowing terms and ideas is crucial opposed to other 254 classes. Methodical learning is not the best bet because her exam questions can be very tricky.
Straight forward class, if you read textbook/ppt slides and do all the problems you should be good.

All she does is reads off the slides, and many people are bored due to that. If you are capable to keep your focus even when a professor is literally reading word for word the you should choose this professor.

Dr. Tan is a great lecturer. She is able to explain important details of the slides. Also, she is always prepared. She has made her own slides to fit with what she wants to teach. If you practice all of her practice/homework questions and understand key concepts, the class will be not bad.

Do all the tutorial and practice problems and you will do very well in her class. I thought she did a very good job lecturing as she was concise and clear. Also, she seemed to be very knowledgeable on the subject. I would take the class with her again.

Lecture slides were easy to understand but were uploaded on canvas. She basically reads the lecture slides but understands what she's teaching. Do the readings and a lot of practice on homework questions and you should do fine.
Boring lecturer hardest midterm and final I've taken.
not a bad professor at all

Lectures could be a bit better.  Often time you hear a bunch of words and phrases all rushing into ur ears but u have no idea what he was talking about.  Anyways Nice Prof!  He likes to joke around sometimes, which makes the lectures a little less boring. Do ALL ur assignments and Redo them a few times to get good marks. Pretty Tough Exams!

Pretty nice professor.  The materials are alright, but the last part of the course can be difficult.  The exams and marking were fair as a whole.  If you are going to take 254, Spector is a decent choice.
No Comments
read the txtbook if u want a good mark ...lecture, u can go if u hv nth to do...dont' rely on the powerpoints and the exam prep.  the prof is nice ^^

Dont go to the lecture it is a waste of time. He is an extremely nice person though. Read the book 4 times! thats all you need. AND do questions as much as you can. The materials are easy but questions can be confusing.
His exams are fair boz he said he hate tricky questions.So just study his notes and you will be fine.
Nice guy, not very useful lectures. fair exams. Overal he gets a B in teacihng. he is kinda funny too.
No Comments
He never responds to your email, unfair!Even though you got A, but you only learned a little.

He is a well-meaning teacher, he tries to make everyone get a good grade, as long as you keep up with assignments, and do practice exams questions, and online (mcgraw-hill) multiple choice questions you can get an A. I did, and i missed over half the lectures. he's pretty funny too, for an accountant
Simply da BEST! My first ever "A+"
A great prof....just do ur readings and do as many problem, and exercise questions in the text!!
would you like some more crispy creams????????
Agree with last comment.  BUT he's very nice and forgiving because he actually cares about his students.
his nice, friendly, but yet i count on textbook rather than his lecture
He gives you the easiest examples but the tests are way harder!
easy course, funny guy, great class.
He makes an "academically challenging" course comprehensive with his slades and teaching ability. Exams were a little on the difficult side but were fair. Do you homework!
Great prof!
Great Prof.. funny and nice!!! Exam was too long but not very difficult!!  Best lectures ever cause he is soooooooo funny
Great prof. you can tell he spent a lot of time on his lecture slides. exams and final was fair.
nice one
great teacher... exams were fair
No Comments
Very easy course which means that very competitive too. Our class average was 87%.
He was very clear and did not try to trick you purposely.  Great Prof

Arsineh is one of the sweetest professors at SFU! She really cares about her students. Her slides and notes are useful for exams. Midterm and final are fair and based on what is taught in lecture. The textbook is useful for practice questions and clarification if needed.

She is beautiful, very friendly and nice. By attending the lectures, you will know what is expected in the tests. She is very clear about what is important in every lecture. Also, she always asks for students' feedback in order to improve her teaching skills properly. Besides, she gave students Valentine chocolate.  You'll not regret if taking her

She is beautiful, very friendly and nice. By attending the lectures, you will know what is expected in the tests. She is very clear about what is important in every lecture. Also, she always asks for students' feedback in order to improve her teaching skills properly. Besides, she gave students Valentine chocolate :) you'll not regret if taking her

I wish I hadn't chosen her as my prof for BUS 254. She speeds through lectures and you cannot absorb anything therefore it makes going to lectures a waste of timepointless. She does give out examples that are really helpful and will appear on the test. The final was easier than the midterm especially if you do her practice questions many times.

She isn't as easy as the reviews here make her seem. The mid-term was quite long, but the material in it was not super hard. The final was easier and I finished it faster than the mid-term. The lectures are long, but it is because there is just so much material to cover. Study what she points out and you'll do fine.
Truly cares about the students. Teaches very well, is very thorough. Easy to listen to/understand. Material is simple. Plus, she's hot.

easy course. most of the content are based on calculation. Her midterm was a bit tough but final was easy. Key to success in her class is to attend all the classes and listen carefully what questions are going to be on the exams. Yes, she will tell you.
THE BEST PROF EVER!!!!!!!!!!!! she really cares about her student, and tell you exactly what she expected on the exam!!! THE BEST PROF EVER!!!!!!!
BEST. PROF. EVER. the class itself can be difficult if you dont keep up with work but she makes it so much better, she knows how to teach and she is hilarious and CUTE :)

Entertaining and helpful prof. She's adorable during lecture. Tutorial is utterly useless b/c all u do is sit there and listen to students who can't speak english try to present solutions to problems. Unfortunately it's mandatory. Exams are fair, she'll let you know what to expect.The course itself is hard so study study study!
Extremely helpful and caring prof, will go out of her way to accommodate. The material is not hard, textbook and lectures used extensively.

Nice teacher. She makes it pretty clear what will be on  the exams and what isn't very important. She's not very strict with keeping people quiet, which sucks a bit, but if you don't sit in the back you should be fine.

She is very nice! try to get a spot on her tutorial. It is not hard to pass the course,but not easy to get a good grade either (b+ and up). Don't miss lectures as she will say what will appear on the exam. Loved the course and the prof!
Sadly, the 2nd person below, did not study.
Final was really hard!!! But thankfully, she really cares about her students that gave us relateivly high grades compared to other profs

During review session, she stated that students will be tired for the 7-10pm final exam. Therefore, she promised to make it more bearable. Sadly, she didn't live up to the bargain.  I  had nightmares when I slept after the exam.

Easy-going Prof. She gives away candies to keep students motivated. She actually gives hints on what is on the exam in class. Mid-term is easy. Class average is like 70%. Final is considerably difficult. But her class is scaled and her average is a B- but not C+. This course itself is hard, but getting a decent grade in her class is not that hard:)

A quality prof that cares about the subject and her students. It IS a hard subject, but she makes it bearable. Yes, the final is ridiculous but it's a curved class so does it really matter? If you keep up with readings and do the homework each week, it's actually not bad at all. Go to her office hours if you need help and you should be fine.

the Final is the hardest one i've ever met so far in sfu.the textbook is not good at all, u don't have any extra practice for this stupid sfu-published textbook (251 is a really good one), so most of the them would be painful when   they take the final exam. Most of the questions you must really familiar with, however,  you just don;t know how to d

I like her as a person. She is really approachable and friendly. Midterm is O.K. but the final is a heart-breaker. I saw many students came out of the classroom with a stern face. Overall, it's a nice prof but you better study hard.

prof is extremely helpful and very approachable. the course itself is pact with LOTS of info. midterm was bearable, but final....wow ppl got raped 23/90 students failed 25%...that's a huge amount. the final was BRUTAL...though=( overall the prof is great, and i do not think another teacher can do a better job than she does! recommend!!!!!!!!
GREAT BUS PROF I've ever met!!!Love her :) very helpful, strongly recommand

SUPER nice prof, the BEST prof I have ever had in SFU.. she really care about her students n try to help them as much as she can, though it's a hard course dry material but she is the best for BUS254  coz she make it lill   bearable.. she try to give high grade but cant coz of the bus scale.. sheeven get DRUNK 3 days if she give a student F grade !

Great lecturer, tries to make the boring material as interesting as it could be. She hands out free candy!! Reading the textbook and keeping up with the class is 100% essential. Midterm was very difficult. Final is moderate if you prepare. Overall a wonderful, helpful and extremely nice prof.

Not the BEST prof, but a good prof still, for bus 254.  Due to the fact that 254's course material is hard to start with, this course is not easy at all.  But her exams are VERY FAIR, just do all the exercises for every chapter, and understand the steps and procedures, and you will get a good mark.  Attendance is NOT MANDATORY.  Don't procrastinate

She is the nicest professor I have ever had! I went into her office for a quick question and she spent 20 minutes going over other course material with me. She is extremely approachable, very helpful and super nice!!!!!!

AMAZING prof! So much personality makes this boring class bearable. Her exams are hard cuz she has to scale but she gives maximum amount of high grades out as possible. SO helpful, very clear, very approachable, very funny!! One of the best I've had in 2.5 years at SFU
Nice prof. who gives out candy. She tried, just that the course material is too dry and hard. REALLY hard exams.
such a nice prof. explains things extremely well and really cares about her students. def recommend =)
Wonderful lecturer. Very considerate. Always cares about the thoughts of students.
She is nice but the exams are hard. Plus, there's intense competition for grades as it's scaled. For example, only 2% of the class gets an A+.
Very hard prof, super low averages for the class exams. Do not trust if she say easy exam they are both tough.
GOOD PROF GIVES U CANDY COURSE MOFO HARD THO
had easy examples in lecture notes of questions but midterm she asks much harder questions.

I took her class and she was very intelligent. I read other reviews and she never told us what was on the mid terms or the finals but I think what people are trying to say that she teaches and helps on those points. Very helpful and she managed to keep me awake! Amazing teacher, no doubt.
Very very helpful, tells what is exactly on the midterm and final and is VERY fair!!!!! Love her!!!!!

This prof is super nice! This is the first time she teaches at SFU. I hope she will be here to teach in the future. She tells you exactly she she expects for MT and final and formats of the questions look alike to our practice questions!
She is amazing!! so nice, helpful and kinda funny. she always makes time for questions and tells you exactly what she expects you to know for midterm/final. shes the best!!
shes so nice! very very helpful. encourages questions and extremely helpful. thats all i can say.

She was the best prof I had in SFU. Explains concepts well and gave good examples.  Exams might be tough considering she emphasizes writing down every single unit but the course was overall, not too challenging if you put in the effort.
She is a great prof! If you attend class, pay attention, and stay on top of the homework you will be fine. Super helpful professor. Lectures have a lot of content but slide have lots of examples.

She tried a little too hard to be popular with the students dropping brand names and talking about trends. She isn't the best speaker but not horrible and tries hard to keep things interesting. Reasonable at explaining things.

She tried a little too hard to be popular with the students dropping brand names and talking about trends. She isn't the best speaker but not horrible and tries hard to keep things interesting.  Reasonable at explaining things.

lectures are straight forward. put in the work and you'll get a good grade. she's sassy and arrogant about her career which is really annoying (tone it down pls). exams are clear and she tells you what is on them basically.

Sure, she taught how to be succeed in personal life and she reply the email quickly. But personally, i don't like this woman, she is so pround of her career and her life.  she is impatient to response student academic question, and  exercise problem.

If you're willing to put in the effort to study and succeed in life then I HIGHLY recommend her class. Her classes are informativeengaging and she genuinely cares about her students. I really enjoyed coming to her classes because she gives a lot of life lessons and how to succeed in life. She's def. someone I aspire to become in the future.
She explains materials so well and gives you formulas she summarized for each chapter. Easy A, as long as you do the homework. It's kind of hard to fail this class if you do the work, really
She's so hot loll. Very helpful and tells you exactly what to study for from her practise questions in class. Recommended!
She might not be good at teaching the content clearly, but her exams are fair and quite easy as long as you pay attention in classes and do the assignments.

Great instructor + fair marker. She is very good at what she does, and is proud of her accomplishments- for those that actually strive to excel and be successful, don't be afraid to approach her for advice as she is very much willing to help. Just understand and practice the weekly assignments & you'll ace the exams. Highly recommended.
She goes fast and does not record her lecture, she is open to feedback and will help if you ask for it

Pros: 
 -Midterm is very easy
 -She lectures clearly and coherently 
 -She is quite attractive 
 Cons:
 -Final is very hard
 -She has very limited office hours
 -She is very sassy and can be quite condescending at times

She spends a lot of time trying to be cool, both by brand reference, and by embarrassing students (not me, so this isn't me retaliating).  Lectures are a bit fast, but she tries to keep it interesting.  Examples used in class directly relate to exam, and she does a good job pointing out what to expect on the exam.  Marking seemed fair.

Very good prof. Would recommended. First midterm is easy. The final is harder but OK. She makes her lectures interesting and focuses on what is most important. Comment that said she's not good for telling people to go home makes it sound worse than it was. She just said that because she emphasized reading that chapter since it's the most important

I recommend her because she chooses only the important questions to do for homework and not pointless ones to waste our time. She explains lectures very well. Tutorials are a MUST to attend for marks and a presentation in it. Exams are pretty fair.
She makes the lecture super interesting. She explains the material extremely well and makes it simple compared to the textbook. Great prof!

She told us to "Just go home if you didn't read the chapter on ABC costing.. you wont understand anything in lecture today" Gee, I wonder what that says about her ability to teach the material... I regret taking this class with her, she definitely gives off a sassy vibe. Would recommend you to choose another prof.

One of the nicest, most approachable, funny, and kind profs I've ever had. He speeds through lectures but he ALWAYS makes time for his students if they need help, even if he is always extremely busy. His exams are tricky but very fair. You have to put a lot of time into the material to do well, but he's an amazing prof.
He is GOOOOD! I screwed up my midterm (only 67) and he really gave his students a second chance! My final grade for this course is A+. The lectures are helpful and detailed. Highly recommended!

I didn't attend any tutorials or did any homework. I crammed for one day before the midterm and one day before the final and got a C+. This guy can teach! Simple efficient clear concise, and lets you off early when he's done. I only needed a passing grade.
Tough class, tough teacher ... if you don't work hard.  Derek is fair and flexible, and fairly easy to understand.  Just pay attention to details.

Class is somewhat boring, but that's expected. Derek explains material fairly clearly. Must do assignments and practice problems. Class is curved and everyone does so well that it's difficult to get a good grade.

really need to do the hw and textbook exericises in order to do well in class. class is very competitive, so hard to get a good grade. reasonable exams however, as long as you do the homework and understand the concepts. boring class, and answers to the hw are not discussed in tutorial nor posted

It's not a hard course, exams were reasonable, but beware who are you taking with, since everyone did soo well.. a good grade will not be easy, but I think he wont really fail anyone if you just work fine. Alternative: Steven Spector
Lectures are boring.  Work on the assigned and additional problems and you'll do ok on exams.  Exams are easy but too long since you are less likely to have enough time to doublecheck your work.

The MT and Final isn't hard at all (75% avg for MT) but it is hard to get good grades because everyone is doing so well, he explain the concept very well and very helpful in webct. Do the assignments, they are very helpful
Derek is very helpful and clear. Lecture is a bit boring tho. Exams are fair. Really have to do the homework assignment yourself in order to get good grade in this course.
The problems in class and assignments are relatively easy.  However, the final and midterm are horribly hard, without any practice final or midterm available.  Horrible long boring lectures as well.
Good personality, tried to help me out during office hours.  The final was relatively difficult but it's a difficult class.  His lectures are VERY boring, and unncessary if you can read the textbook.
No Comments

final exam was VERY difficult-you have to REALLY understand the course in order to get a good mark.there was a lot of complaint from the class b/c we only learn the basics in tutorial and in hw. he doesn't even give sample exams! class was boring, course is boring. good luck.
A pretty bad prof. Sure, he is clear and the tests are generally easy, but he is really boring and mono-toned. He also does not actively try to help outside of class.
The asian version of the teddy bear.

solving the additional homework practice questions, in class tutorial questions, slides would be your best help for this course. The exams are completely based on the above, tutorial participation + in class group quiz matter, so don't miss out on those. Overall easier than BUS 251 for sure.
I really recommend Teresa! Do the homework and score well on the midterm and you'll be alright!

ofc the contents were tough tho, i would say her course was 10/10.
exams were very straightforward, if you do homework and additional homework well, you'll be fine with the exams. 
lectures were not mandatory, I also didn't go often, but tutorials were mandatory. they were inclass team quizzes and homework checking by TA.

She is an excellent professor who ensures the material is easy to understand. Her approach simplifies learning and makes it easier to follow along. Attending her lectures is highly beneficial, as she explains all the concepts thoroughly and at a comfortable pace, significantly reducing the need for extensive self-study.

She was just reading off the slides the whole time. She did try to give examples, but the fact that she spoke at 10 words per second made it less than ideal to follow. Basically she was just rapping tbh

Overall course structure is very straightforward. Midterm worth 25%, final worth 50%, and the remaining 25% spread across tutorial participation, tutorial assignments, and homework checks in tutorial. Course work is heavy on theory and calculations. If you read slides/textbook and do practice questions/homework you'll do good.

Coming to lectures was pointless as you could not understand anything in class and it was boring due to her monotone voice. You have to teach yourself and there is a lot of content to grasp. The final was worth 50 percent, making or breaking your grade. To do well study every week and watch yt videos as the slides and lectures did not help.

Matt is a really nice and caring professor, and he gives as much material as he can to prepare you, but the pros of taking a class with him stop there. His voice is monotonous and his lectures are him reading off the textbook notes. Tutorials - homework answers read to you. Get ready to read and practice everything yourself.

Matt isn't the greatest professor. His lectures are rather pointless as they are taken directly from the textbooks. Tutorials are useless as well as you just get homework answers read to you. Exams were pretty fair. He is a nice guy but his ability to teach is rather limited.

Masood is a great professor. He is very helpful and approachable if you need extra instruction. He is very encouraging to students who are doing well (but kind of condescending to under performers). His tests are hard but fair, you just really have to study hard and read the textbook. He tells you whats on the final during lectures.

Masood is my least favourite prof at SFU. You're going to have to learn the whole course on your own because him going through slides published by the book, really isn't that helpful. Also he says what COULD POTENTIALLY be on the exam which is completely useless because anything could be on the exam. My biggest regret is taking 254 with Masood.

He uses the publisher's ppts. Talks for long periods of time, then blazes thru 10 slides in the blink of an eye. Only talks about theory in class, never goes over actual problems. If George is your TA then prepare to learn the course on your own cause all he does is waste time in tutorial. 254 with this prof is a total rip off of your tuition!

A helpful prof that genuinely cares about his students. Lectures can seem a bit dry - thats Accounting. The TAs are what will carry you through the semester. Midterm is similar to homework questions, but harder. Final is similar to HW. Review, review, review!
skipped almost all lectures, all i did was keep re-doing the textbook questions he assigned ( at least 3 times for each chapter), ended up with B+

The material is not easy, but he emphasized contents which he thinks are important for the exam! He's a nice guy, and has a great attitude towards the course! He's willing to explain all the problems you have with the material!
He's a good prof. The content is just difficult. Keep up with readings and do the assignment questions = A. All of you people blaming him, suck it up and stop being disrespectful.
Pretty nice prof! Midterm and final is not very hard!!! As long as you are familiar with material, it'll be fine.

He emphasized the important material in class, i.e. he spent more time talking about the topics covered on the exam. This guy did not like asking questions in class and did not encourage students to participate, which was one of the reasons why BUS254 was boring, dull and dry.
He is a very boring teacher, please dont take morning classes with him. Half of the final exam questions were vague with multiple possible answer.Exams are very very Hard.

Good prof, definitely not my favorite class in terms of content, BUT he was helpful.  He did his best to explain things, but it really comes down to how much you time you put in. His assignments are very helpful to prepare for the exams, and his practice/review questions are a great tool!

Average prof. Super nice guy though. His answers to the homework questions are in a bizarre format (multiple unnecessary pages), and having to do presentations at his whim can be a little tough--be prepared to present on the first day, get a 10/10 and get it out of the way. Also, go to class, he slips in hints about the exams.

Hard class and hard professor. The best part of the class was looking at all of his clip-art in the lecture slides. There was seriously no subject where he didn't find an appropriate piece of clip-art.
Boring lecture and hard final.

His classes are always in the morning at 8:30 am. This makes it hard for some to stay awake such as myself. The notes barely correlate with the homework assignments. In fact they are 10 times harder. The textbook is long and wordy. He also fails 12% of the class. I am really freaking out for the final now. This is a hard and stressfull class.

The prof was great! The course content, not really my field of interest. However, he was good at explaining, and if you go to all the lectures and tutorials, and actually try to learn, you will get a nice A- like me.
do not take 254 with him! Super tricky and long midterms/finals and he is impossible to understand!
most boring prof! talks like a machine. extremely hard exams, and plus, % of midterm and finals are extremely high too!  Do NOT take any course with him!
He talks like a radio, non-stop and exam is very hard
Do not go with Best SFU tutor. A ripoff.

his lectures will make you so bored you will want to cry...he doesn't put any effort in making any of the course material interesting. the midterm is definitely more tough than the final so make sure you know your stuff

Accent is hardly the issue. Prof is articulate but utterly useless as a prof. I almost lost faith in SFU as an inst. of higher ed. Should have waited 1 semester. Never again. Worst course I have taken.  No wonder tutors making $. Also, no correlation btwn lectures and exams. If this is what being a CMA is abt, thx, but no thx. This has implications

Fair prof - exams can be lengthy and tricky, but at the end you get curved up. MT was a bit twisted, but no surprises on the final. Rushes through slides, but emphasizes on some imp topics that show up on exams so attend lecture for that. I found his "accent" super clear for obvious reasons.
